post.description:
Revoke the current device token and issue a fresh one.

Accepted auth: static API key OR an existing (non-expired, non-revoked) device token.
The new token is returned once — store it securely.

Desktop/Updater melden ihre installierten Versionen und Update-Channel.

Auth: Device Token (preferred) oder statischer API-Key (legacy).

get.description: Docker container status (requires /var/run/docker.sock mounted).
Host-System Metriken (CPU, RAM, Disk, Uptime).
Public plan list for home portal pricing/checkout.

post.description:
Publish a new Stripe Price for the plan and migrate all active subscriptions
to it (Mode A: new price takes effect at next renewal – proration_behavior=none).

Prerequisites:
- plan.amount_cents must be set
- plan.is_active must be True
- STRIPE_SECRET_KEY must be configured

get.description: Return Stripe product/price info and active subscription count for a plan.

post.description: Revoke a license (POST action variant; canonical endpoint).

get.description: Read-only data snapshot for Figma Make. Protected by X-Preview-Key header.
Full device detail including versions and online status (JWT auth).

patch.description: Override the update channel for a device (stable|beta|internal).

post.description: Generate license keys – canonical POST endpoint (replaces legacy POST /ui/licenses).

Unblock a license using state-machine rule:
- 'activated' if activated_at is set and (expires_at is null or expires_at > now)
- 'issued' otherwise

get.description: List devices activated for the current home portal user (via their licenses).

patch.description: Rename (set host_name) on a device owned by the current home user.

delete.description: Revoke device: clears activated_device_install_id from the user's license.

patch.description: Patch expiry date (and optional notes) on a license.

get.description: List all feature overrides (read: any console role).

post.description:
Create or update a feature override (admin only).

Uniqueness key: (feature_key, scope, COALESCE(target_id, '')).
Emergency kill-switch: upsert global override with enabled=False, rollout_percent=0 (admin only).

get.description:
Returns the current license status including plan details and feature flags.
Accepts either:
  - Bearer <keycloak_access_token>  (desktop client, home portal)
  - X-Api-Key / X-Agent-Api-Key     (agent service, fallback)
When a Bearer token is provided the device_install_id must belong to the
authenticated user (sub claim), preventing cross-user license probing.

post.description:
Create a Stripe Checkout Session for a given plan.
Returns the checkout URL the frontend should redirect to.
Create a Stripe Customer Portal session for billing management.

Handle Stripe webhook events.
Validates signature, processes checkout.session.completed and subscription events.

get.description:
List tickets.

- Any authenticated user (incl. pcw_user): returns own tickets only.
  Uses GET /api/v1/tickets/search?query=customer_id:<id> with fallback to
  customer.id:<id> for cross-version compat.
  If caller has no Zammad account yet: returns [] (no user creation side-effect).
- Elevated (pcw_admin/pcw_support/pcw_console/owner/admin) + ?all=true:
  returns all tickets via GET /api/v1/tickets (no scope filter).
- pcw_user + ?all=true: 403.

parameters.name: all

schema.description: Admin only: return all tickets, not scoped to caller
schema.title: All

parameters.description: Admin only: return all tickets, not scoped to caller

schema.title: Response List Tickets Api V1 Support Tickets Get

post.description:
Create a support ticket.

Uses Zammad's "customer": "<email>" field directly — Zammad resolves or
provisions the customer internally. No manual user-create, no roles lookup.
article.internal is explicitly false so the customer can read the article.

schema.title: Response Create Ticket Api V1 Support Tickets Post

get.description:
Get a single ticket.

- pcw_user: 404 if ticket.customer_id != own Zammad user ID (no info leak).
  If caller has no Zammad account: 404 (no user creation).
- Elevated roles: no ownership check.

schema.title: Response Get Ticket Api V1 Support Tickets  Ticket Id  Get

post.description: Reply to a ticket via Zammad ticket_articles (supports optional attachments).

schema.title: Response Reply Ticket Api V1 Support Tickets  Ticket Id  Reply Post

post.description: Upload helper: returns a base64 attachment object compatible with ticket_articles.

schema.title: Response Upload Attachment Api V1 Support Attachments Post

get.description:
Admin-only: list all Zammad roles (id, name, active).

Use this to verify whether role ID 3 is "Customer" on this Zammad instance.
ZAMMAD_CUSTOMER_ROLE_ID is informational only and no longer used in the
self-service flow; this endpoint exists for operational verification.

Equivalent curl:
  curl -s "$ZAMMAD_BASE_URL/api/v1/roles" \
       -H "Authorization: Token token=$ZAMMAD_API_TOKEN" \
       | jq '[.[] | {id, name, active}]'

Unknown: Zammad GET /api/v1/roles requires admin-level API token.
If this returns 403, your ZAMMAD_API_TOKEN lacks admin scope.

schema.title: Response Diag Zammad Roles Api V1 Support Admin Diag Zammad Roles Get

get.description:
Admin-only: search Zammad for a user by email.

Shows whether users/search matches for a given email and what customer_id
would be used for ticket scoping. Same lookup as GET /tickets (own scope).

Equivalent curl:
  curl -s "$ZAMMAD_BASE_URL/api/v1/users/search?query=<email>" \
       -H "Authorization: Token token=$ZAMMAD_API_TOKEN" \
       | jq '.[0] | {id, email, login, role_ids}'

schema.title: Response Diag Zammad User Api V1 Support Admin Diag Zammad User Get

post.description: Inbound webhook from Zammad. Verified via shared secret.

schema.title: Response Zammad Webhook Api V1 Support Webhook Post
